text.title: Architecture Decision Process Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure applying the decision-tree-process methodology end-to-end. Each step lists inputs, action, output, and optional decision gate.

step.name: phase1-problem-definition
step.action: State as a single question, list out-of-scope, classify reversibility.
step.decision-gate: Vague question or missing reversibility → STOP.

step.name: phase2-options-generation
step.action: Diverge to ≥5 options including do-nothing + unconventional; converge to 2-5 viable shortlist with elimination rationale.

step.name: phase3-tradeoff-analysis
step.action: Build weighted matrix; stakeholders set weights; evidence-based scoring; name at least one criteria conflict.
step.decision-gate: Weights not summing to 100% OR scores without evidence → fail.

step.name: phase4-validation
step.action: For one-way doors: design POC with success criteria; run; assess. For all: stakeholder review.

step.name: phase5-documentation
step.action: Compose ADR with status, context, options considered, decision, consequences, review_trigger, review_date.

step.name: phase6-implementation
step.action: Generate implementation task list + monitoring + rollback plan.
step.output: Tasks in tracker + dashboards + calendar reminder.
